questions.question: Who is the runaway slave Paul is sending back to Philemon?
questions.feedback: Paul appeals on behalf of Onesimus, whom he converted while in prison.

questions.question: Paul says he is sending Onesimus back as what?
options: No longer a slave but a beloved brother
questions.correct: No longer a slave but a beloved brother
questions.feedback: Paul asks Philemon to receive Onesimus no longer as a slave but as a beloved brother.

questions.question: Paul commands Philemon to accept Onesimus back.
questions.feedback: Though Paul could command it, he appeals instead on the basis of love.

questions.question: Paul says if Onesimus owes Philemon anything, to charge it to _____.
questions.feedback: Paul tells Philemon to charge any debt to his own account.

questions.question: The name Onesimus means _____.
questions.feedback: Paul makes a wordplay: Onesimus (meaning useful) was formerly useless but is now useful.
